mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.5%, or signal loses predictive power with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real final sales combined with stable or declining inflation indicates robust demand growth without monetary tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return frequency above 60% when RSAFS increases and CPIAUCSL growth stays below 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization improvements and earnings revision upgrades, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns in greater than 50% of observed cases during confirmed expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with quarterly forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts indicates collapsing construction demand and tightening credit conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of cases following a monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation alongside stagnant or rising federal funds rates forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and high-rate regime yields positive quarterly returns in greater than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms strong aggregate demand and supports corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during stable macro regimes.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a frequency exceeding 58% in walk-forward validation.
